pi_items.source: Confirmed via setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts which reference this exact path. This is a standard Linux home directory location for user 'ga' with standard LibreCAD documents subdirectory.

AutoCAD Color Index (ACI) standard: color code 7 = White. Confirmed via official AutoCAD documentation and AUGI article on AutoCAD's 7 named colors. LibreCAD uses the same ACI standard as documented in gohtx.com/acadcolors.php.

AutoCAD Color Index (ACI) standard: color code 2 = Yellow. Confirmed via official AutoCAD documentation and multiple sources including AutoCAD Help and AUGI resources. LibreCAD uses the same ACI standard.
AutoCAD Color Index (ACI) standard: color code 5 = Blue. Confirmed via official AutoCAD documentation and multiple authoritative sources. LibreCAD uses the same ACI standard.
AutoCAD Color Index (ACI) standard: color code 3 = Green. Confirmed via official AutoCAD documentation and multiple authoritative sources. LibreCAD uses the same ACI standard.

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims verified. The parking_lot_striping task is a synthetic, generative task where an agent creates a parking lot drawing from architectural specifications in LibreCAD. The expected output file is a DXF file at /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/parking_lot.dxf. Four layers must be created with specific names and AutoCAD Color Index (ACI) codes: LOT-BOUNDARY (white/7), STRIPING (yellow/2), ADA (blue/5), and DIMENSIONS (green/3). All color codes match the official AutoCAD/LibreCAD standard color palette. LibreCAD is a legitimate, open-source 2D CAD application that natively supports DXF format with layer management and color assignment.
